description: Corridors used for patient movement in new healthcare occupancies must be a minimum of 8 feet wide.
All corridors serving as means of egress must remain clear and unobstructed with minimum 8ft width at all times.
Operating rooms must have a minimum of two means of egress accessible from the suite.
ICU patient areas must be arranged so patients can be evacuated horizontally to adjacent smoke compartments.
Waiting areas must have unobstructed egress paths with a minimum clear width of 44 inches.
Operating rooms must have a dedicated medical gas system with O2, N2O, medical air, CO2, and vacuum outlets meeting NFPA 99 Category 1 requirements.
ICU patient care areas must have Category 1 medical gas systems including oxygen, medical air, and vacuum at each bed position.
Pharmacy areas handling flammable materials must be separated from other areas by fire-rated construction.
Emergency department treatment areas must have Category 1 medical gas and electrical systems with emergency power backup.
Patient rooms must be equipped with an addressable fire alarm initiating device (smoke detector or pull station within 200ft).
Operating rooms must have smoke detection connected to the building fire alarm system with automatic notification to staff.
Sterile processing areas must be equipped with automatic sprinkler systems per NFPA 13 light hazard occupancy classification.
Clinical laboratories must be equipped with automatic sprinkler systems classified as Ordinary Hazard Group 1 or 2.
Mechanical rooms must be separated from occupied areas by minimum 1-hour fire-rated construction.
Exit stairways must be enclosed in 2-hour fire-rated construction and have minimum 44-inch clear width.
